 {"id":"bd-1t881","title":"Test: Unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching against actual agent output, covering all rate limit variations.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/limit_detector_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestLimitDetector_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    
    // Test all known rate limit patterns
    patterns := []struct {
        name       string
        agentType  string
        output     string
        shouldMatch bool
        matchType   string
    }{
        // Claude Code patterns
        {
            name:       "claude_rate_limit_standard",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Error: rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_429_error",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "API Error 429: Too Many Requests",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "429",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_token_limit",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "You have exceeded your token limit for this billing period",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "token_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_normal_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "I'll help you implement that function. Let me start by...",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        
        // Codex patterns
        {
            name:       "codex_rate_limit",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "openai.RateLimitError: Rate limit reached for default-gpt-4",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "codex_quota_exceeded",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Gemini patterns  
        {
            name:       "gemini_resource_exhausted",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "google.api_core.exceptions.ResourceExhausted: 429 Resource exhausted",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "resource_exhausted",
        },
        {
            name:       "gemini_quota_limit",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "Quota exceeded for GenerateContent",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Edge cases
        {
            name:       "partial_match",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "The word rate appears but limit does not follow immediately",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        {
            name:       "case_insensitive",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "RATE LIMIT EXCEEDED",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "multiline_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Processing...\nWorking...\nError: rate limit exceeded\nRetrying...",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "empty_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range patterns {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Agent: %s", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Output: %q", truncateForLog(tt.output, 100))
            t.Logf("Expected match: %v", tt.shouldMatch)
            
            match, pattern := detector.CheckOutput(tt.agentType, tt.output)
            
            t.Logf("Result: match=%v, pattern=%q", match, pattern)
            
            if tt.shouldMatch {
                assert.True(t, match, "Should detect rate limit")
                assert.NotEmpty(t, pattern, "Should identify pattern type")
                if tt.matchType != "" {
                    assert.Contains(t, pattern, tt.matchType, "Pattern type mismatch")
                }
            } else {
                assert.False(t, match, "Should NOT detect rate limit")
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_CapturePane(t *testing.T) {
    // Create mock tmux client
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureResponses: map[string]string{
            "cc_agents_1:1.0": "Normal agent output here",
            "cc_agents_1:1.1": "Error: rate limit exceeded",
            "cc_agents_1:1.2": "", // Empty pane
        },
    }
    
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        paneID      string
        expectLimit bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", false},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.1", true},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.2", false},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.paneID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Capturing pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            output, err := detector.CaptureAndCheck(tt.paneID, "cc")
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("Captured output length: %d", len(output))
            t.Logf("Limit detected: %v", tt.expectLimit)
            
            match, _ := detector.CheckOutput("cc", output)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectLimit, match)
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_BackgroundMonitoring(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{}
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    detector.pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("Starting background monitoring")
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 200*time.Millisecond)
    defer cancel()
    
    eventCh := detector.Events()
    
    // Start monitoring
    go detector.StartMonitoring(ctx, []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc"},
    }, "test_session")
    
    // Inject rate limit after short delay
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(75 * time.Millisecond)
        mockTmux.SetCaptureResponse("test_session:1.1", "rate limit exceeded")
    }()
    
    // Wait for event
    select {
    case event := <-eventCh:
        t.Logf("Event received: %+v", event)
        assert.Equal(t, "cc", event.AgentType)
        assert.Contains(t, event.Pattern, "rate")
    case <-ctx.Done():
        t.Log("Context cancelled before event (expected if timing varies)")
    }
}

// Helpers
func truncateForLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log each test case input (agent type, output snippet)
- Log expected vs actual match results
- Log pattern type when matched
- Log pane capture details
- Log background monitoring events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test all known Claude Code limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Codex limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Gemini limit patterns
- [ ] Test case insensitivity
- [ ] Test multiline output
- [ ] Test empty output
- [ ] Test partial/false matches
- [ ] Test pane capture integration
- [ ] Test background monitoring
- [ ] Mock tmux client for isolation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:54.929888923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608090689Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608007314Z","close_reason":"Added LimitDetector tests (all patterns, multiline, capture, monitoring); go test ./internal/swarm -run LimitDetector","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3un3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1t8p","title":"EPIC: Local LLM Support via Ollama and llama.cpp","description":"## Overview
Add support for local LLM backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio) as first-class agent types, enabling privacy-sensitive work, offline operation, and cost-free experimentation.

## Problem Statement
Current NTM only supports cloud LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini). Users need:
- Privacy for sensitive codebases (air-gapped environments)
- Offline operation when internet unavailable
- Cost-free experimentation and learning
- Running specialized/fine-tuned local models

## Key Features
1. New agent type: --local=N or --ollama=N for spawning local agents
2. Model selection: --local-model=codellama:34b
3. Mixed fleets: combine cloud and local agents in same session
4. Performance monitoring: track token/s, memory usage
5. Automatic model management: pull models on demand

## Technical Design
1. Ollama adapter:
   - Connect to local Ollama server (default: localhost:11434)
   - Map NTM commands to Ollama API
   - Handle streaming responses

2. llama.cpp adapter:
   - Direct integration for llama.cpp server mode
   - Support GGUF model loading
   - GPU acceleration detection

3. Agent type hierarchy:
   - CloudAgent (existing): Claude, Codex, Gemini
   - LocalAgent (new): Ollama, LlamaCpp
   - All agents implement same interface

## Configuration
- NTM_OLLAMA_HOST for custom Ollama URL
- NTM_LOCAL_MODEL for default model
- Per-project config in ntm.yaml

## Success Criteria
- Can spawn local agents alongside cloud agents
- Local agents participate in normal workflows
- Performance metrics visible in dashboard
- Works fully offline (after model cached)

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/agent/local.go (new - local agent implementation)
- internal/agent/ollama.go (new - Ollama adapter)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (add --local flags)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/agents.go (show local agent metrics)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:25.999766320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:48:22.570133773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1tb8","title":"Task: Add automatic model management for local LLMs","description":"## Overview
Enable NTM to automatically pull and manage local LLM models when needed.

## Deliverables
1. Model management commands:
   - ntm models list - show available local models
   - ntm models pull <model> - download model from Ollama registry
   - ntm models remove <model> - delete model to free space
   - ntm models recommend - suggest models based on hardware

2. Automatic pull on spawn:
   - If --local-model not found locally, offer to pull
   - Show download progress in terminal
   - Continue spawn after model ready

3. Model recommendations:
   Based on available VRAM:
   - <4GB: codellama:7b-instruct, deepseek-coder:6.7b
   - 4-8GB: codellama:13b, deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct
   - 8-16GB: codellama:34b, deepseek-coder:33b
   - 16GB+: mixtral:8x7b, codellama:70b

4. Storage management:
   - Show model sizes and disk usage
   - Warn when disk space low
   - LRU cleanup suggestion for unused models

## Implementation Notes
- Use Ollama API for all model operations
- Cache model metadata locally for fast lookup
- Support offline operation with cached models

## Acceptance Criteria
- Model commands work correctly
- Automatic pull flows smoothly
- Recommendations match hardware
- Disk space warnings appear appropriately
- Unit tests for model management","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:23.066483753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:45.145324634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- palette.go: `loadPaletteCommands` (file I/O)
- quota.go: `runQuota` (I/O)

 {"id":"bd-1tmw5","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command","description":"Add robot mode command to manually trigger account switch.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-switch-account claude         # Switch to next Claude account
ntm --robot-switch-account openai account3  # Switch to specific account
ntm --robot-switch-account --pane agent-1 claude  # Switch for specific pane
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "switch": {
    "success": true,
    "provider": "claude",
    "previous_account": "account1@email.com",
    "new_account": "account2@email.com",
    "panes_affected": ["agent-1", "agent-2"],
    "cooldown_seconds": 300
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Parse provider and optional account name
2. Call CAAM switch
3. Update affected pane environments
4. Return result JSON

## Code Location
- internal/robot/switch_account.go
- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

## Testing
- Switch to next account, verify output
- Switch to specific account
- Switch with pane filter
- Switch when no accounts available","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:52:03.353964001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289374984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289301996Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-switch-account command for CAAM account switching. Added PrintSwitchAccount in robot package, flag registration in cli/root.go, and unit tests. Supports switching by provider or provider:account with optional pane filter.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yty1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-26185","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-27l8q","title":"rano: Implement PID-to-pane mapping for agent attribution","description":"Create mechanism to map process PIDs to NTM pane identities.

## Challenge

rano sees PIDs, NTM sees panes. Need to bridge:
- Pane "agent-1" has shell PID 1234
- Agent process (claude, codex) is child of shell
- May have multiple child processes (node, etc.)

## Implementation

### 1. Get Shell PID per Pane
```go
func GetPanePID(session, pane string) (int, error) {
    // tmux list-panes -t session:pane -F '#{pane_pid}'
    cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "list-panes", "-t", fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", session, pane), "-F", "#{pane_pid}")
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    // Parse PID
}
```

### 2. Get Child Processes
```go
func GetChildPIDs(parentPID int) ([]int, error) {
    // Read /proc/<pid>/task/<tid>/children
    // Or use pgrep -P <pid>
}
```

### 3. Build PID Map
```go
type PIDMap struct {
    paneToMainPID   map[string]int      // pane -> shell PID
    pidToPane       map[int]string      // any PID -> pane
    mu              sync.RWMutex
}

func (m *PIDMap) Refresh() {
    for pane := range m.paneToMainPID {
        shellPID := getShellPID(pane)
        childPIDs := getChildPIDs(shellPID)
        // Update maps
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/rano/pidmap.go

- Verify child process discovery","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:14:39.397643370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:16:22.448544109Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:16:22.448465561Z","close_reason":"Implemented PID-to-pane mapping in internal/integrations/rano/pidmap.go with comprehensive unit tests. Provides PIDMap struct with refresh logic, child process discovery via /proc, and bidirectional PID-to-pane lookups for rano network attribution.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27l8q","depends_on_id":"bd-308f5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27l8q","depends_on_id":"bd-33wzo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-28cpd","title":"PromptInjector: Handle Codex double-Enter quirk","description":"## Purpose
Handle Codex-specific quirk that requires double-Enter to submit prompts, ensuring reliable prompt injection across all agent types.

## Implementation Details

```go
// AgentQuirks defines agent-specific prompt submission behaviors
type AgentQuirks struct {
    DoubleEnter    bool   // Codex requires double Enter
    EscapePrefix   bool   // Some agents need Escape before input
    ClearLineFirst bool   // Clear existing input before prompt
}

var agentQuirksMap = map[string]AgentQuirks{
    "cod": {DoubleEnter: true},
    "cc":  {DoubleEnter: false},
    "gmi": {DoubleEnter: false},
}

// SendWithQuirks sends prompt handling agent-specific requirements
func (pi *PromptInjector) SendWithQuirks(paneID, agentType, prompt string) error {
    quirks := agentQuirksMap[agentType]
    
    pi.logger.Debug("applying agent quirks",
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "double_enter", quirks.DoubleEnter)
    
    // Clear line if needed
    if quirks.ClearLineFirst {
        if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "C-u", false); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("clear line: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    // Send the prompt text
    if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, prompt, true); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send prompt: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Handle Codex double-Enter quirk
    if quirks.DoubleEnter {
        pi.logger.Debug("sending extra Enter for Codex", "pane", paneID)
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond) // Brief pause between Enters
        if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "", true); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("send second enter: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    pi.logger.Info("prompt sent with quirks applied",
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType)
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log which quirks are being applied per agent type
- Debug-level log for double-Enter sends
- Info-level confirmation on successful send

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Codex prompts reliably submitted with double-Enter
- [ ] Quirks map is extensible for future agent types
- [ ] Small delay between Enters to ensure reliable submission
- [ ] No quirks applied to cc/gmi agents
- [ ] Unit tests verify quirk application per agent type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:39.308715362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:40:03.952997406Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:40:03.952294280Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28cpd","depends_on_id":"bd-17zih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28cpd","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28n0d","title":"Implement shell environment detection","description":"Implement shell environment detection.

## What

Detect shell type and common configuration that affects agent operation.

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "shell": {
    "type": "zsh",              // bash, zsh, fish, etc.
    "tmux_plugin_detected": true,  // May cause issues
    "oh_my_zsh_detected": true,    // Common zsh framework
    "config_path": "~/.zshrc"    // Where to look for aliases
  }
}
```

## Detection Methods

### Shell Type
- Read $SHELL environment variable
- Verify against running shell process

### tmux Plugin Detection
Check common plugin locations:
- ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/tmux
- ~/.zshrc contains 'plugins=(... tmux ...)'
- ~/.tmux.conf sourced plugins

### oh-my-zsh Detection
- Check for ~/.oh-my-zsh directory
- Check ZSH environment variable

## Why This Matters

tmux plugins can:
- Alias the tmux command
- Add automatic session attachment
- Modify key bindings

Agents need to know to use /usr/bin/tmux directly when plugins detected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.601068157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787668899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787582285Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28n0d","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28o9m","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing reasoning modes","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing/browsing reasoning modes

## Command Structure
```
ntm ensemble modes [subcommand] [flags]

Subcommands:
  list    List modes (default)
  show    Show full details for a single mode
```

## List Command
```
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., Formal, Causal)
  --tier string       Filter by tier (core|advanced|experimental)
  --all               Alias for --tier all (show all tiers)
  --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose           Show full descriptions + failure modes
```

### Default Behavior
- **Default list = core tier only** (curated).
- If advanced modes exist, print a hint: `Use --all to show advanced modes`.

## Show Command
```
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id|code> [flags]
  --format string     Output format: text, json, yaml (default: text)
```

## Behavior
- `ntm ensemble modes` with no subcommand defaults to `list`.
- Accepts lookup by **mode id** (lowercase) and **mode code** (e.g., A1).
- JSON/YAML outputs include: id, code, tier, name, category, short_desc, outputs, best_for, failure_modes, differentiator, icon, color.

## File Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog loader (bd-qn9gc)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default list shows only core tier
- [ ] Category + tier filters work
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs include code + tier
- [ ] `modes` defaults to `list`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:23.818764532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069282507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069258051Z","close_reason":"Implemented as 'ntm modes' command (not 'ntm ensemble modes'). Functionality complete: ntm modes list with --category, --tier, --all, --format flags; ntm modes explain for mode details. All acceptance criteria met.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28o9m","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3h0n","title":"Task: Create timeline panel widget for dashboard","description":"## Overview
Implement the visual timeline panel that renders agent activity as horizontal bars.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/tui/dashboard/panels/timeline.go:
   - TimelinePanel struct implementing Panel interface
   - Horizontal bars per agent showing state over time
   - Color coding: Idle=gray, Working=green, Waiting=yellow, Error=red
   - Time axis at bottom with tick marks

2. Rendering logic:
   - Calculate pixels per time unit based on panel width
   - Render each agent as a horizontal track
   - Fill bars with state colors for each time period
   - Add labels for agent names on left

3. Visual design:
   Agent-1: [████░░░████████░░███████░░░░░░████]
   Agent-2: [░░░░████████░░░░░░░░████████░░░░░░]
   Agent-3: [████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░███████]
            |-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|
            10:00 10:15 10:30 10:45 11:00 11:15

4. Panel controls:
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys
   - Scroll left/right with arrow keys
   - Jump to now with 'n' key
   - Show event details on hover/select

## Implementation Notes
- Use lipgloss for colored block rendering
- Efficient rendering (only render visible time window)
- Support variable panel width

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timeline renders correctly in dashboard
- Colors match state semantics
- Zoom and scroll work smoothly
- Panel adapts to width changes
- Unit tests for rendering logic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:50.199826149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:52:03.473303629Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:52:03.473236923Z","close_reason":"Timeline panel implemented: TimelinePanel struct with horizontal bars per agent, color coding (Working=green, Waiting=yellow, Error=red, Idle=gray), time axis with tick marks, zoom (+/-), scroll (arrows), jump to now (n), details (enter). 14 unit tests pass. Visual design matches spec.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h0n","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h0n","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3h8q","title":"E2E Tests: YAML config driven swarm startup","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of starting swarm from YAML config file.

## Test Scenario
1. Create swarm.yaml with full config
2. Run ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml
3. Verify agents match config
4. Verify prompts match config
5. Verify assignment settings applied

## Test Config (test_swarm.yaml)
```yaml
session: e2e-config-test
agents:
  claude: 2
  codex: 1
options:
  no-user: true
prompts:
  init: "Test initialization prompt"
assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 3
```

## Verification
- Session name matches config
- Agent count matches config
- Init prompt sent to all agents
- Assignment limit respected

## Test File
